source: Tonietto & Carbonneau 2004; Bisztray et al.; Tarcal Research Institute drought-tolerance literature
notes: Tokaj-centric; Tarcal literature emphasises efficient stomatal control and drought tolerance on rhyolite/loess. huglin_max widened from 3300 to 3700 and winkler_class_max raised to 4 to keep Tokaji inside envelope under end-century RCP8.5.

source: Hungarian Wine Marketing Agency variety profiles; Tokaj producer notes
notes: Late-ripening Tokaji partner of Furmint; envelope widened to mirror Furmint's heat tolerance.

source: OIV Muscat blanc à petits grains profiles
notes: Treated as Muscat blanc à petits grains; high sugar accumulation, prone to rot in wet years.
Modern Tokaj crossing — bands cloned from Hárslevelű.
Bands cloned from Furmint with slightly earlier ripening.

source: Old indigenous Tokaj variety, recently revived
notes: Very limited data; bands assumed similar to Hárslevelű.

source: Tonietto & Carbonneau 2004; widely planted across Central Europe
notes: Most planted white in Hungary; tolerates a wide climate range, performs best on calcareous and volcanic soils.
Cool-climate variety; loses aromatic finesse above ~2200 Huglin. Frost-hardy buds.
Early ripening, cool-climate; sensitive to drought.
Synonym for Rizlingszilváni; same envelope.
Versatile across cool to warm sites; on Badacsony makes powerful styles.
Aromatics fade in hot sites; prefers cool, late-ripening conditions.
Wide adaptability; aromatic profile shifts with heat.
Most widely planted international white; very adaptable.
Cool-climate Pinot family; similar envelope to Chardonnay but narrower upper limit.
Cool-continental variety; Sopron and Etyek are at the southern edge of its comfort zone.

source: Hungarian Wine Marketing Agency; Badacsony Wine Society
notes: Female-flowered, low-yielding Badacsony specialty; bands tracked to Badacsony's projected warmer climate.

source: Hungarian Wine Marketing Agency; Somló producer notes
notes: Somló indigenous; thrives on volcanic basalt. Frost-sensitive in spring. Heat envelope nudged warmer to track Nagy-Somlói projections.
Hungarian crossing; hardy and disease-tolerant; envelope inferred from parent varieties.
Very early ripening aromatic; grown across the Great Plain; prone to spring frost.
Old Transylvanian variety; cool-climate aromatic white. Limited published envelope.
'Little Princess'; selected from Leányka. Bands inferred from Leányka.

source: Hungarian Wine Marketing Agency; Mór district notes
notes: Móri specialty; frost-prone, late-ripening. Sparse published data.

source: Austrian Thermenregion references; Pécs producer notes
notes: Late-ripening aromatic; grown almost exclusively in Pécs in Hungary. Thermenregion is a warm-continental site, so upper bound nudged warmer.
Hungarian crossing for the Great Plain; very little published climate data.
Disease-resistant Hungarian PIWI; heat-tolerant; widely planted on the sands.

source: Hungarian Wine Marketing Agency; Kunság cellar notes
notes: Late-ripening Kunság specialty; tolerates sandy, hot sites. Sparse data.

source: Hungarian Wine Marketing Agency; Carpathian basin variety lists
notes: Old Carpathian-basin variety; very limited published climate envelope.
Early ripening Muscat; aromatic; sensitive to drought and heat.
Most planted red in Hungary; mid-late ripening, frost-tolerant buds, prefers cool nights.

source: Hungarian Wine Marketing Agency; Szekszárd & Eger producer notes
notes: Late-ripening, thin-skinned; frost-sensitive in spring; needs warm, dry sites.
Villányi flagship; mid-late ripening, broadly adaptable, more cold-tolerant than Cabernet Sauvignon.
Late-ripening; needs warm summers; struggles in coolest Hungarian districts.
Mid-ripening; widely adaptable but sensitive to spring frost.
Cool-climate red; aromatic finesse fades above ~2200 Huglin.
Warm-climate variety; only Villány and Szekszárd reliably ripen Syrah in Hungary.
Austrian crossing (St-Laurent × Blaufränkisch); cold-hardy, mid-ripening.

source: Hungarian Wine Marketing Agency; Austrian/German references
notes: Early-ripening light red; widely grown in Villány historically as Kékoportó.

source: Tonietto & Carbonneau 2004; OIV Tempranillo profile; Ribera del Duero references
notes: Spanish Rioja/Ribera flagship; warm, drought-tolerant, mid-late ripening. winkler_class_max raised to 5 and heat_tolerance_days raised to 28 to reflect Iberian summer heat-day baselines.

source: OIV Touriga Nacional profile; Douro references
notes: Portuguese Douro flagship; very heat- and drought-tolerant. Douro routinely sees 35-50 heat days >35C; tolerance_days=32 reflects native climate.

source: Tonietto & Carbonneau 2004 HI+1; OIV Grenache profile
notes: Mediterranean classic; HI+1 thermal class. heat_tolerance raised to 32 to reflect Châteauneuf/Aragon summer heat baselines.

source: Tonietto & Carbonneau 2004 HI+2 'Very Warm'; Bandol/Châteauneuf-du-Pape/Jumilla references
notes: Very late-ripening Mediterranean; HI+2 'Very Warm' class. huglin_min raised from 2200 to 2700 and opt_low to 2900 — must reach Tonietto's HI+2 (2700-3000) to score, excluding all cool northern Hungarian districts. heat_tolerance 32 reflects Provence/Jumilla baselines.

source: Tonietto & Carbonneau 2004; OIV Carignan profile; Languedoc references
notes: Heat- and drought-tolerant; old-vine Mediterranean workhorse.
Southern Italian; very late-ripening, structured tannins.

source: Tonietto & Carbonneau 2004 HI+3 'Very Very Warm'; Sicilian references
notes: Sicilian extreme-heat red; HI+3 (>3000) classical thermal class. huglin_min raised from 2200 to 2800 and opt_low to 3000 — exceeds projected end-century Huglin even of the warmest Hungarian districts (~2960), so it should only score modestly in the 2-3 hottest sites. heat_tolerance 35 reflects Sicilian summer heat baselines.
Tuscan flagship; mid-ripening; broadly adaptable warm-climate red.
Heat- and salt-tolerant white; aromatic; key Mediterranean white candidate.
Greek extreme-heat white; retains acidity at very high temperatures. heat_tolerance raised to 32 to reflect Santorini summer baselines.
Southern Italian heat-tolerant aromatic white.
Spanish Rueda heat-tolerant white; aromatic and acid-retaining.

source: OIV Albariño profile; Rías Baixas references
notes: Galician humid-warm white; Atlantic-influenced; tolerant of heat with sufficient rainfall.

source: Geilweilerhof / Freiburg PIWI breeding program references; widely grown in DE/CH/UK/Scandinavia
notes: PIWI disease-resistant white (Geilweilerhof crossing, 1975). Extremely early-ripening, cool-climate. Bands modeled on Müller-Thurgau / Riesling cool envelope. Added per validation audit.

source: Freiburg PIWI breeding program; widely grown in southern Germany / South Tyrol
notes: PIWI disease-resistant white (Freiburg). Cool-climate, early ripening. Riesling-like envelope. Added per validation audit.
PIWI white with Riesling parent; cool to mildly temperate. Added per validation audit.
PIWI grey-skinned white; mid-ripening, more heat-tolerant than Solaris/Bronner thanks to Cabernet sauvignon parent. Added per validation audit.
Aromatic PIWI white; cool to temperate; Muscat-style aromatics. Added per validation audit.
PIWI disease-resistant red with Cabernet sauvignon character. Mid-ripening, more cold-tolerant than its Cabernet sauvignon parent. Added per validation audit.
